commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 408 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 408's PureTech 1.2L and PHEV 1.6L engines dominate replacement enquiries, with wet belt degradation and hybrid system faults being the most prominent concerns. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific variant.

problems.h4: PureTech Wet Belt Failure - Why EB2ADT Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 408 Active Premium, Allure, GT (2022-2025, EB2ADT, HNK, EB2DTS)
problems.rootCause: The wet timing belt runs in oil and can degrade prematurely, releasing debris that blocks the oil pump pickup and causes catastrophic lubrication failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wet belt, tensioner, oil pump filter before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full oil system flush, belt replacement, sump removal and cleaning
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if debris detected but no bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated belt spec, upgraded components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if oil starvation has occurred; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 408 worth £20,000-£30,000, preventative wet belt replacement at 60k miles is far more economical than waiting for failure. If you're hearing rattles or have oil pressure warnings, replacement with a rebuilt unit featuring the updated belt specification is the safest long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2ADT engine prices for your Peugeot 408

problems.h4: PHEV Hybrid System Integration Fault - Why EP6FDT Units Need Specialist Attention
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 408 Allure PHEV 180, GT PHEV 225 (2022-2025, EP6FDTM, EP6FDTX)
problems.rootCause: Complex communication between petrol engine, electric motor, and e-EAT8 transmission can fail due to software calibration drift or component wear, triggering hybrid system warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, ECU reflash, hybrid system relearn
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable for software-related faults; not mechanical

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty e-motor coupling, inverter, or control module
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if fault isolated to single component; requires PHEV specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt hybrid powertrain with battery health check, system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for multiple faults or high-mileage PHEV; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: PHEV systems demand specialist diagnostics. If your Peugeot 408 shows hybrid warnings alongside reduced electric range, get a specialist assessment before committing to repair. For units over 60k miles, a rebuilt PHEV engine with updated integration components often offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EP6FDT Peugeot 408 PHEV engine replacement

problems.h4: Turbocharger Fault on PureTech 130 - Performance Loss & Oil Leaks
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 408 GT, GT Premium (2022-2025, EB2DTS, HNK)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger wastegate actuator sticks or oil seals degrade under high-load driving, causing boost loss, blue smoke, or oil consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator, recalibrate boost control
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if turbo core is healthy; quick fix for boost issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean, replace seals/bearings, balance turbocharger
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if housing is undamaged; extends turbo life significantly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if turbo failure caused secondary engine damage

problems.recommendation: If your GT-spec 408 has lost power but no oil consumption, actuator replacement may suffice. However, if you're seeing blue smoke or metal particles in the oil, the turbo has likely contaminated the engine - a rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2DTS engine prices for your Peugeot 408 GT

problems.h4: Piston Ring Wear on PureTech - Oil Consumption & Engine Damage
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 408 Allure, Allure Premium, GT (2022-2025, HNK, EB2ADT)
problems.rootCause: Early PureTech variants can experience piston ring sticking due to carbon buildup from wet belt debris or short-trip driving, leading to excessive oil consumption and potential cylinder scoring.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, clean carbon, replace piston rings
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if cylinders are undamaged; requires skilled machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded rings, updated belt spec, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil consumption exceeds 1L/1,000 miles or compression loss detected

problems.recommendation: Piston ring wear on the PureTech is rarely a simple fix. If your Peugeot 408 is consuming oil rapidly or has failed an emissions test due to blue smoke, replacement with a rebuilt engine featuring upgraded ring design is the most reliable solution.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned HNK Peugeot 408 engine with warranty

problems.h4: e-EAT8 Transmission Fault on PHEV - Hybrid Drivetrain Issues
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 408 Allure PHEV, GT PHEV (2022-2025, EP6FDTM, EP6FDTX)
problems.rootCause: The e-EAT8 hybrid transmission can develop clutch pack wear or software calibration errors, causing harsh shifts, loss of electric mode, or hybrid system warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, transmission adaptation relearn, software flash
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for calibration-related faults only; not mechanical wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove transmission, replace clutch assembly, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if fault isolated to clutch; requires PHEV-certified specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt hybrid unit with updated e-EAT8, battery health check
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for multiple faults or high-mileage PHEV; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: e-EAT8 faults on the 408 PHEV demand specialist assessment. If your hybrid is losing electric mode or showing transmission warnings, get a PHEV-certified diagnostic before committing to repair. For units over 60k miles, a rebuilt powertrain often provides better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Enquire about EP6FDTX Peugeot 408 PHEV engine availability

Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 408 and engine code. keyChanges: New fastback body style introduced with EMP2 platform
PureTech EB2ADT 1.2L turbo petrol launched at 130hp
PHEV variants with EP6FDT 1.6L hybrid system debuted at 180hp/225hp
e-EAT8 automatic transmission standard across range

HNK - 1.2L petrol turbo calibrated variant (Allure / GT)
knownFor: Early PureTech units show wet belt degradation concerns by 60k miles
PHEV integration faults reported on high-mileage examples
Strong demand for replacement engines as early adopters reach service intervals
Specialist rebuilders developing updated wet belt solutions

faq.h2: Peugeot 408 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 408 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Peugeot 408 PureTech rebuilt engines typically cost £1,900-£2,900 supply-only, while PHEV variants range £2,800-£4,500 depending on engine code and trim level.
keyPoints: PureTech EB2ADT/EB2ADTD: £1,900-£2,900 rebuilt supply-only
PHEV EP6FDT variants: £2,800-£4,500 rebuilt supply-only
Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on location
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 408 engine replacement prices today

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot 408?
items.answer: Peugeot 408 uses PureTech codes EB2ADT, HNK, EB2ADTD, EB2DTS for petrol variants and EP6FDTM, EP6FDTX for PHEV models from 2022-2025.
keyPoints: PureTech: EB2ADT (base), HNK (calibrated), EB2ADTD (updated belt)
PHEV: EP6FDTM (180hp), EP6FDTX (225hp performance)
Enter registration above to verify exact code for your variant
Engine code determines compatibility and replacement pricing
items.cta: Check your Peugeot 408 engine code instantly

items.question: Is a reconditioned Peugeot 408 PureTech engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, when sourced from specialists who verify wet belt specification and include upgraded components addressing known failure points with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units must have wet belt spec verified or updated
Look for suppliers offering upgraded piston rings on PureTech
Minimum 12-month warranty essential for peace of mind
Avoid cheap used engines without full history verification
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Peugeot 408 PureTech engine with warranty

items.question: How long does Peugeot 408 PHEV engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only PHEV engines typically dispatch in 3-5 working days; supply & fit appointments require 1-2 days at a certified hybrid specialist garage.
keyPoints: PureTech engines: 2-4 days supply-only, 1 day fit
PHEV engines: 3-5 days supply-only due to calibration requirements
Supply & fit appointments scheduled within 7-10 days nationwide
PHEV installations require hybrid-certified technician
items.cta: Book Peugeot 408 PHEV engine supply & fit today

items.question: Can I replace my Peugeot 408 PureTech engine myself?
items.answer: Not recommended - PureTech wet belt calibration and PHEV hybrid system coding require specialist diagnostic equipment and technical expertise.
keyPoints: Wet belt tension requires factory-spec tools and calibration
PHEV systems need high-voltage safety certification to service
ECU coding essential post-installation for emissions compliance
DIY attempts may void warranty and cause secondary damage
items.cta: Get quotes from certified Peugeot 408 engine specialists

items.question: What causes Peugeot 408 PureTech engine failure?
items.answer: Wet belt degradation is the primary cause, releasing debris that blocks oil pickup and causes lubrication failure, typically between 60k-90k miles.
keyPoints: Wet belt debris contaminates oil, blocking pump pickup
Secondary failures: piston ring wear, turbocharger damage
Updated EB2ADTD specification reduces failure risk significantly
Preventative belt replacement at 60k miles recommended
items.cta: Compare rebuilt PureTech engines with updated belt spec

items.question: Do Peugeot 408 replacement engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on all rebuilt/recon units
PHEV warranties include battery health verification
Warranty terms vary by supplier; confirm before purchase
Supply & fit packages often include labour warranty
items.cta: View Peugeot 408 engine warranty options from UK suppliers

items.question: How do I find my Peugeot 408 engine code?
items.answer: Check the vehicle registration document (V5C), engine bay plaque, or enter your reg above for instant DVLA-verified code identification.
keyPoints: V5C document lists engine code in section D.2
Engine bay plaque shows code near firewall or strut tower
DVLA lookup via registration gives instant verification
Engine code essential for accurate replacement pricing
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 408 engine code now

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot 408 engine?
items.answer: Yes, for 2022+ fastback models valued £20k-£30k, rebuilt engine replacement at £1,900-£4,500 offers strong value versus vehicle replacement costs.
keyPoints: Rebuilt PureTech: £1,900-£2,900 vs £25k+ new vehicle
PHEV replacement preserves hybrid efficiency and tax benefits
Updated-spec rebuilt engines address original failure points
Minimum 12-month warranty protects your investment
items.cta: Calculate if Peugeot 408 engine replacement makes financial sense

items.question: Can I upgrade my Peugeot 408 engine during replacement?
items.answer: Limited upgrades possible - PureTech units can receive updated wet belt spec; PHEV variants require exact code match for system compatibility.
keyPoints: PureTech: Upgrade to EB2ADTD updated belt specification
PHEV: Must match exact EP6FDT variant for e-system compatibility
Performance mapping upgrades available post-installation
Consult specialist before considering non-standard swaps
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 408 engine upgrade options
